scheme_name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) - Central Sector Scheme
short_description: Merit-based scholarship for students from class 11 to postgraduate level with family income below ₹4.5 Lakhs.
detailed_description: The Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students is a merit-based scholarship scheme for students pursuing regular courses at college and university level. The scheme provides financial assistance to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies. The scholarship amount ranges from ₹10,000 to ₹20,000 per annum depending on the course level.
key_benefits: Direct transfer to student's bank account
income_criteria: Family income not exceeding ₹4.5 Lakhs per annum
other_conditions: Must have secured at least 80% marks in Class 10 and 12 for fresh scholarship
Must be enrolled in regular full-time courses
Not more than 2 children of same parents can avail scholarship
Students pursuing distance/correspondence courses not eligible
Must maintain satisfactory academic performance for renewal

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: For undergraduate and above, proof of 80% marks
Family income certificate not exceeding ₹4.5 Lakhs per annum
Student's bank account for scholarship transfer
Current year fee receipt from institution

application_steps_online: Visit National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Register as new user with valid email and mobile number
Verify email and mobile through OTP
Login and select 'Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship'
Fill application form with personal, academic and income details
Upload all required documents in specified format
Submit application and save application ID
application_steps_offline: Obtain application form for Central Sector Scholarship
Fill form completely with accurate information
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents
Institution will verify and forward to authorities

submission_locations.notes: Online application preferred, institution will verify documents

faq.question: What is the minimum percentage required?
faq.answer: Students must have secured at least 80% marks in Class 10 and 12 examinations for fresh scholarship.

faq.question: Can I apply if I'm in a private college?
faq.answer: Yes, students in both government and private recognized institutions are eligible.

faq.question: Is the scholarship renewable?
faq.answer: Yes, if you maintain satisfactory academic performance and continue in eligible courses.

common_rejection_reasons: More than 2 siblings already receiving scholarship
tips_for_successful_application: Ensure all marksheets show at least 80% marks
Get income certificate from competent authority
Keep application ID safe for tracking
